name: plugin-dev
description: Lean, security-aware authoring kit for Claude Code plugins and the shared cross-host core of the coder-plugins platform-authoring family, with a balanced three-way determinism boundary: mechanical work goes to a deterministic bash validation suite (scripts/validate-plugin.sh orchestrating per-domain validators that emit a shared JSON finding contract) plus scaffolders, while judgment splits by where it must run — judgment-interactive (interviews, confirm/approval gates) stays in skills/commands on the main loop since a dispatched agent cannot run AskUserQuestion, and judgment-batch-isolated (heavy fan-out, model-pinnable work) is carved into agents only on a concrete benefit. The deterministic lane also covers the artifacts a plugin persists: schema-versioned formats scanned with per-artifact/per-tier ceilings and fixtures, plus depth tiers for elastic skills. The same boundary is portable: install-kit.sh + scaffold-validator.sh vendor a self-contained deterministic lane (the shared contract + a generic orchestrator + generated domain validators) into ANY plugin, the determinism-boundary skill teaches the split (with references for skill-to-agent extraction, tiered artifacts, and depth tiers), and the create-plugin / refactor-plugin commands make new and existing plugins balanced like plugin-dev itself. Skills for plugin structure / skill / command / agent / hook / MCP authoring, cross-host subagent scaffolding (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode), leak-audit and best-practices-sync passes (the latter also auditing harness-assumption scaffolding for re-testing as models improve), plus skill-workshop which mines session JSONL history (via the session-analyzer Haiku subagent) for skill candidates; agents for validation, skill review, agent generation, and session mining. Current to June 2026: 32 hook events, five handler types, userConfig/channels/dependencies manifest fields, monitors/bin/LSP surfaces. Platform-specific authoring lives in sibling plugins: cowork-dev, cursor-dev, codex-dev, opencode-dev, hermes-dev, openclaw-dev.
